description: Well-known sources for AI policy file conventions. Maintained by aipolicy.1mb.dev.

sources.name: AGENTS.md Specification
sources.description: The AGENTS.md convention for AI coding agent instructions. 60k+ repos. Stewarded by the Agentic AI Foundation under the Linux Foundation.

sources.name: Linux Foundation Generative AI Policy
sources.description: Linux Foundation policy on AI-generated contributions: licensing compliance, attribution, and third-party content handling.

sources.name: OpenSSF Guide for AI Code Assistant Instructions
sources.description: Security-focused guidance for configuring AI coding assistants. Covers secure coding prompts, supply chain safety, and language-specific rules.

sources.name: ASF Generative Tooling Guidance
sources.description: Apache Software Foundation policy on AI-generated contributions. Requires license compatibility and recommends Generated-by commit trailers.

sources.name: Open Source AI Definition (OSAID)
sources.description: OSI's canonical definition of Open Source AI (v1.0, Oct 2024). Establishes the four freedoms: use, study, modify, share.

sources.name: CNCF: Sustaining Open Source in the Age of Generative AI
sources.description: How CNCF projects handle AI contributions. Covers shared governance patterns, disclosure, and maintainer capacity.

sources.name: Open Source AI Contribution Policies
sources.description: Curated catalog of 50+ real-world AI contribution policies across open-source projects. Categorized by stance: accepting, restricting, or rejecting.

sources.name: Kubewarden AI Policy
sources.description: CNCF project policy requiring Assisted-by trailers, human accountability, and engagement with reviewers.

sources.name: CloudNativePG AI Policy
sources.description: Structured AI policy prohibiting unvetted boilerplate, hallucinated features, and shotgun refactoring.

sources.name: Kyverno AI Usage Policy
sources.description: Comprehensive policy with PR limits, disclosure trailers, and rules scoped to accepted issues only.
